content.text: Enterprise LLM agreements carry obligations that extend well beyond the signature date — permitted use categories, data-handling requirements, seat or call-volume limits, SLA credits, and renewal triggers all require continuous tracking. Without systematic compliance monitoring you learn about violations only after vendors audit you or after an invoice surprises. This topic builds a practical compliance tracking system from data collection to audit evidence.
Enterprise agreements typically create obligations across four dimensions:
Seat counts, API call volumes, token budgets per tier
No-training opt-out active, DPA countersigned, residency enforced
Regulatory violation; audit finding; contract breach
Named users only, role-based provisioning, no credential sharing
License audit liability; security incident scope expands
Uptime measurements taken, credit claims submitted within 30 days
Credits forfeited; no leverage at renewal
Define a canonical schema that maps each obligation to a measurable signal.

Most LLM vendors expose usage through a billing API or cost management console. Tag every request at the gateway layer with a cost-centre label so you can slice consumption by team, project, and model before it reaches the vendor statement.

Named-user licenses require periodic reconciliation against your IdP. Pull active users from your identity provider and diff against the vendor's provisioned user list. Orphaned accounts (departed employees still provisioned) are both a compliance gap and a security risk.
Data Processing Agreements create specific obligations that cannot be measured through usage APIs — you need a checklist-based verification run on a regular cadence.
Vendor notification email logged + CMDB updated
Incident runbook includes vendor notification step
Test erasure request completed within 30 days

Uptime SLA credits require you to measure incidents independently, file claims within the window (commonly 30 days from incident), and reconcile credits on invoices. Most organizations forfeit credits because no one owns the claim process.
Configure three alert severity levels so that every notification reaches the right owner with enough lead time to take corrective action.
Usage >80% of limit or soft threshold
Usage exceeds limit or SLA claim deadline <48h

Auditors (SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR supervisory authorities) require contemporaneous evidence that controls operated continuously, not reconstructed screenshots. Automate evidence capture as part of the nightly compliance run.

import json, hashlib
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

EVIDENCE_DIR = Path("/var/compliance/evidence")

def save_evidence(
    control_id: str,
    report: "ComplianceReport",
    raw_data: dict,
) -> Path:
    """Write immutable evidence record for a compliance control execution."""
    timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
    payload = {
        "control_id": control_id,
        "execution_time": timestamp,
        "report_summary": report.summary(),
        "raw_data": raw_data,
    }
    content = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, default=str)
    digest = hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    path = EVIDENCE_DIR / f"{control_id}_{timestamp}_{digest}.json"
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(content)
    # Make file read-only to preserve immutability
    path.chmod(0o444)
    return path
Maintain a central register of every enforceable obligation from all active agreements. Update it whenever a contract is signed, amended, or renewed.
Path to most recent audit artifact
Automated quota, seat, and data-residency checks; alert routing
Review P2/P3 alerts; clear false positives; update obligation statuses
Full compliance dashboard review; file any SLA credit claims; reconcile invoices
Obligation register review; update for contract amendments; assess new tools
Pre-renewal compliance posture review; audit evidence package assembly

Enterprise agreement compliance is an ongoing operational function, not a one-time check. Model every contractual obligation as a measurable signal, collect evidence automatically through ETL pipelines, route alerts by severity to accountable owners, and preserve immutable audit artifacts from day one. The organisations that handle vendor audits confidently are those that run compliance checks continuously rather than assembling evidence retrospectively when an auditor arrives.
